static_proof.merge_train_timing_proof: median-of-3-real-trials timing comparison (train vs. simulated serial baseline built from the UTV2-1461 decision packet's own 2N-3N-CI-cycle cost model) reproduced across 4 separate runs (1 isolated + 3 full-suite pnpm test/verify runs): ratios 0.275, 0.284, 0.286, 0.279 — all comfortably under the required < 0.5 acceptance threshold. Not a live 3-PR GitHub board (merging real PRs is outside this lane's authority this session, and PM's implementation directive explicitly allowed a controlled/simulated comparison in that case), but a real measured wall-clock comparison, not a narrative estimate.

queries.description: live row-count read against zfzdnfwdarxucxtaojxm (select count(*) from picks) to confirm Supabase connectivity and monitored-table health for this T1 lane, in addition to the full apps/api/src/database-smoke.test.ts pnpm test:db run; this lane's diff (scripts/ops/merge-wrapper.ts, scripts/ops/ops-merge-wrapper.ts, and docs) touches no product runtime, pick pipeline, or Supabase write path, so this is an environment-health proof rather than a feature-behavior proof — matching the pattern used by prior docs/ops-only T1 governance lanes (e.g. UTV2-1494)

runtime_proof.note: pnpm test:db run green: 7/7 pass, 0 fail, 0 skipped. Merge-train's own functional 'runtime' is GitHub API/CI interaction, not Supabase; that surface was validated via a CLI smoke test (pnpm ops:merge-wrapper merge-train --candidates-file <2-entry JSON> --dry-run), which returned exit code 0, code 'merge_train_dry_run', correctly acquired and released the shared merge mutex, and left .ops/merge-lock.json in 'released' status — confirming merge-train is a real, operator-invokable pnpm ops:merge-wrapper subcommand, not merely a library function. A live multi-PR GitHub board smoke test was not performed: this session holds no merge authority (PM standing constraint) and none of this lane's own PR is mergeable yet.

criterion_1.text: Two green PRs can be queued/batched and merged without operator-driven serial update-branch cycles
criterion_1.status: implemented and unit-tested (buildFakeRunner-driven happy-path test drains a 3-candidate batch end-to-end: pr-update-branch -> CI wait -> executor-result re-post -> pr-merge, per candidate, with zero idle gap between candidates, under one mutex hold)
criterion_1.verified_by: scripts/ops/ops-merge-wrapper.test.ts: 'merge-train: happy path drains all candidates and releases the lock'

criterion_2.text: Required contexts still enforced on the actual merge SHA
criterion_2.status: unchanged by design — ci.yml, merge-gate.yml, executor-result-validator.yml, and p0-protocol.yml are untouched; merge-train's pr-update-branch step produces the same synchronize event GitHub has always required a real CI run for. See docs/05_operations/WORKFLOW_SPEC.md 'Merge mechanics' for the explicit per-workflow no-change rationale.
criterion_2.verified_by: static reasoning + docs/05_operations/UTV2-1461-merge-queue-decision-packet.md; no workflow files touched in this diff (confirmed via git diff --stat)

criterion_3.text: Measured: a board of 3 green PRs merges in under half the wall-clock of the serial baseline
criterion_3.status: measured via a controlled/simulated timing comparison (PM-approved substitute for a live board), median of 3 real trials each side, reproduced across 4 separate runs, ratios 0.275-0.286 (< 0.5 required in all cases)
criterion_3.verified_by: scripts/ops/ops-merge-wrapper.test.ts: 'merge-train timing: a 3-PR batch completes in under half the simulated serial baseline (real measured wall-clock)'
